## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, we're gonna start talking about book launches in the same place we started talking about regular launches back in product launch formula and that's with goals. Because unless you know what you want out of your book launches, you can't really design the launch properly to get you where you wanna go. So just like with a regular launch for a product, your strategy is gonna be dictated by what you want to get out of it. There's this saying, if you don't know where you're going, any road will take you there. The thing is, is if you take any road, you're not gonna get where you wanna go. And so that's why you need to design a strategy around your goals. So the question is, why are you writing a book? What do you wanna accomplish with that book? I mean, just getting a book done, that's a big accomplishment, but it's better if it gets you somewhere, if it gets you where you wanna go. So your answer to why you're writing the book and what you wanna accomplish will drag your launch strategy. So for example, my goals with my launch, well, these are my primary goals. I wanted the additional positioning authority of being an author, specifically a bestselling author. Specifically, a New York Times bestselling author. So that would give me additional positioning and authority because sort of where I'm going in my career is this ever bigger arc and ever bigger impact and sort of breaking out of this online marketing world into the larger business world. And so I wanted that additional positioning and authority. I also wanted a new lead source. I wanted a new way to bring leads into my business. So the book was very much about bringing people, getting awareness outside of my smaller marketing, getting awareness out in the bigger world. I also, it was about legacy, frankly. And I hope this doesn't sound too egoful, but I created something really cool. Practical and formula has literally changed the way stuff is sold online. It's changed the marketing. My friend, Evan Pagans, said it's one of the most important marketing innovations in the last 100 years. I don't know if I would go that far, but it was a pretty big deal. It's really changed marketing. It's changed the way stuff is sold. It's changed the way people are able to get their message out into the world. It's changed. People went from, where now people with real expertise or knowledge, they're able to get, build crazy, big, impactful businesses in a very short period of time where they weren't able to do that before. And so since I created this thing, I figure, well, sooner or later, someday when they're writing the history books, someone is gonna get credit for this. And I figure since it was mine, I created it. I invented it. I might as well be the one that gets credit for it. So in one way, you know, writing this book was sort of the way to put this out to the world that, yes, this is my baby, this is my creation.

## [03:02 - 06:05]

So there was a part of legacy here as well. I also just wanted to be able to make a bigger impact. A lot of people can't afford product launch formula. So this is sort of the way to get the product launch formula in my work out for the masses in a way that lots of people could get. And one thing you want to note is that making money was not on my list here. This wasn't about making money or at least money initially. Of course, a new lead source was going to be a way for me to make money long term, but it wasn't like I was writing a book and planning on selling a lot of books and making a lot of money from book royalties. So that led to, so those are my overall goals for the book. So that led to my goals for the book launch where that I wanted to have it be a New York Times bestseller, I wanted to be a New York Times bestseller. I wanted to be on the bestseller list. And hopefully I wanted to be on the New York Times list for more than a single week. I wanted to be there for more than one week because then I thought it could develop some legs and start to develop some real gravity and start to sell on its own beyond my promotions. I also wanted to make money for my partners. So I knew that I was going to be using my partners in this launch, so I wanted to be able to, I wanted to make money for them. Anytime my partners are involved, I want to make money for my partners. And I wanted to have the overall process be profitable. And frankly, if I got those first three goals and it wasn't profitable and I lost some money on the initial thing, I knew that if I achieved those top three goals, then in the long run, I would make money because I would start to generate those additional leads. And all these were based on me having some really strong assets in my business. So I didn't, I just didn't wake up one morning and said I want to be a New York Times bestseller just pie in the sky that that's a cool goal to have. You know, it could be that you just, you always wanted to write a book. Or it could be you want to make a lot of money from your book. ## [06:05 - 07:25]

this teaching world, this information world, this knowledge entrepreneurial world, then, you know, there's a lot of ways to package up your expertise. And if the book is the first thing you're doing, it's probably not going to make you as much money as if you took that same knowledge and you built it into a knowledge product. If you built it into an online product, if you turn it into an online training, if you turn it into a live event, if you turn it into a webinar, if you did a seed launch with it. You know, money, you know, books can build your business, but you rarely, very, very, very few people make a substantial amount of money, make a living wage just from publishing books, especially if it's just one book. So if it's just money that you're after out of this book and you think you're gonna make a lot of money from royalties, a lot of money from selling the book, then there probably are better ways to do that with the same knowledge. Okay, so that's just an overview of some possible goals. I'm sure I've left some off the list here. You might have some goals that aren't on the list here, but yeah, there are some of the big ones. And it's important that you know what your goals are before you start your launch process.
